title: Albert Hopoate (rugby league, born 2001)

[[Albert Hopoate (rugby league, born 1985)|Albert Hopoate]] (uncle) }} }} '''Albert Hopoate''' (born 25 February 2001) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[NRL]]. ==Background== Hopoate is of [[Demographics of Tonga|Tongan]] descent through his father [[John Hopoate]] who was an [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian]], [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan]] and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative.{{Cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/nrl-news-albert-hopoate-to-make-nrl-debut-manly/e0f7a60c-1463-412b-95fb-a8145623cd2d|title=Manly to unleash freak with insane highlights reel|website=wwos.nine.com.au}} His elder brothers [[William Hopoate]] and [[Jamil Hopoate]] also play rugby league professionally, for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Brisbane Broncos]] respectively. Albert played his junior rugby league for the [[Beacon Hill Bears]], before being signed by the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]. ==Playing career== ===Early years=== Hopoate rose through the grades at the Manly-Warringah club, playing for their [[Harold Matthews Cup]] and [[S. G. Ball Cup]] sides, also appearing for the [[New South Wales Under-16's rugby league team|New South Wales under-16s]] side in 2017.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2020/08/31/stats-amazing-albert-hopoate-manly-warringah-sea-eagles-canterbury-cup-nsw-nrl/|title=Stats Amazing | Albert Hopoate|date=31 August 2020|website=New South Wales Rugby League}} He also played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby union team]], New South Wales National Youth [[rugby sevens]] and Australian World Schools rugby sevens teams in 2017.{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/manly-daily/sport/a-new-hopoate-makes-his-mark-in-both-rugby-codes/news-story/55fbfc957d8b15e0a70276d24933775c|title=A new Hopoate makes his mark|date=18 December 2017|website=www.dailytelegraph.com.au}} ===2020=== In August, Manly agreed to loan Hopoate to the [[New Zealand Warriors]] for four games.{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/albert-hopoate-new-zealand-warriors-loan-nrl-signing-news-2020-manly/2hol8ob8rgt1ruldboehfayi|title=Albert Hopoate joins New Zealand Warriors on short-term loan|website=www.sportingnews.com}} A week later, the deal was cancelled after Manly required Hopoate due to an injury to centre [[Moses Suli]]. Hopoate made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Manly-Warringah in round 16 of the [[2020 NRL season]], a 6–30 loss to the [[Melbourne Storm]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2020/round-16/melbourne-vs-manly-warringah/summary.html|title=NRL 2020 - Round 16 - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}} In December, Hopoate signed a one-year contract with the [[Canberra Raiders]] starting in 2021.{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-transfers-2020-albert-hopoate-to-canberra-raiders-manly-sea-eagles-ricky-stuart/news-story/cac0197a38a89a6b518287bc51f80038|title=NRL Transfers 2020: Albert Hopoate to Canberra Raiders, Manly Sea Eagles, Ricky Stuart|date=5 December 2020}} ===2022=== In round 18 against the [[Melbourne Storm]] Hopoate played his first game for the capital club, and the following week against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] he scored his first try as a Canberra Raiders player. Hopoate played seven matches and scored three tries in the [[2022 NRL season]]. Hopoate did not play in either of Canberra's finals games.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-live-parramatta-eels-vs-canberra-raiders-teams-live-scores-brad-arthur-vs-ricky-stuart-highlights-live-stream-joseph-tapine-mitchell-moses-concussion/news-story/ab4febda34065392fcd3071590abc0ec/|title=Eels send ruthless title message, bury finals 'ghosts' in Raiders no-show: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=16 September 2022 }} ===2023=== In round 13, Hopoate scored his first NRL [[hat-trick]] in Canberra's 33-26 victory over [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] at [[Stadium Australia|Accor Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-05-27/nrl-live-updates-warriors-broncos-rabbitohs-raiders/102392212/|title=NRL Saturday: Raiders sneak home in emotional clash with South Sydney, Broncos score upset win over Warriors|website=www.abc.net.au}} Hopoate played a total of 20 matches for Canberra in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 8th on the table and qualified for the finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-canberra-raiders-season-review-jack-wighton-ricky-stuart-jarrod-croker-finals-knights-game-jamal-fogarty/news-story/4befaf00c9ae2de911aed34c6d80c77f/|title=Ricky turned losing a club legend into a winning season. Now for his biggest challenge - Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} ===2024=== Hopoate played nine games for Canberra in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 9th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-canberra-raiders-analysis-exclusive-20240911-p5k9ws.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: The 'wasted' enforcer Ricky Stuart must 'fire up' in 2025|website=www.nine.com.au}} === 2025 === Hopoate would be ruled out for the rest of the season after he tore his ACL during a NSW cup match.{{Cite web |date=2025-03-31 |title=Injury Update: Hopoate, Sanders and Martin |url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2025/03/31/injury-update-hopoate-sanders-and-martin/ |access-date=2025-03-31 |website=Canberra Raiders |language=en}} Hopoate was one of six members of Canberra to depart at the end of their season.{{Cite web |date=2025-09-25 |title=Forever Green: Raiders departing players |url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2025/09/25/forever-green-raiders-departing-players/ |access-date=2025-09-25 |website=Canberra Raiders |language=en}} In October, it was reported that Hopoate is to join the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the super league.{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2025-10-03 |title=Uncontracted outside back set to sign with new club for 2026 |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/uncontracted-outside-back-set-to-sign-with-new-club-for-2026-227461/ |access-date=2025-10-03 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}} On 7 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed a two-year deal with [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=7 Oct 2025|accessdate=9 Oct 2025|title=Warrington sign back Hopoate on two-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cr4qy03vk70o}} ===2026=== Hopoate made his club debut for Warrington in round 1 of the 2026 Super League season against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] which ended in a 24-14 victory.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c70n3gjw4d0o/|title=Taylor-Wray stars as Warrington beat St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} He scored his first try for the club in this victory. He also scored in his second ever Super League game in the 27-16 victory over Wakefield Trinity. == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- |2020 |{{leagueicon|Manly|16}} [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] |5 | | |- |2022 | rowspan="4" |{{leagueicon|Canberra|16}} [[Canberra Raiders]] |7 |3 |12 |- |2023 |20 |8 |32 |- |2024 |9 |2 |8 |- |2025 |1 | | |- |2026 | rowspan=1 |{{leagueicon|Warrington|16}} [[Warrington Wolves]] |2 |2 |8 |- | |Totals |'''44''' |'''15''' |'''60''' |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.raiders.com.au/teams/canterbury-cup-nsw/canberra-raiders/albert-hopoate/ Canberra Raiders profile] {{Warrington Wolves squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hopoate, Albert}} [[Category:2001 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century Australian sportsmen]] [[Category:Australian people of Tongan descent]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Canberra Raiders players]] [[Category:Hopoate family|Albert]] [[Category:Manly Warringah Sea Eagles players]] [[Category:Rugby league centres]] [[Category:Rugby league fullbacks]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Sydney]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Tongan descent]] [[Category:Warrington Wolves players]]
